006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * Represents an HTTP <c>Warning</c> header.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * A general warning about possible problems with the entity body.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
033 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
034 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
035 * (and possibly earlier).
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
038 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * @since 9.2.1
042 */
043public class Warning extends HttpStringHeader {
044
045   /** The header name */
046   public static final String NAME = "Warning";
047
048   /**
049    * Constructor with an eager value.
050    *
051    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
052    */
053   public Warning(String value) {
054      super(NAME, value);
055   }
056
057   /**
058    * Constructor with a lazy value supplier.
059    *
060    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
061    */
062   public Warning(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
063      super(NAME, valueSupplier);
064   }
065
066   /**
067    * Static factory method with an eager value.
068    *
069    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
070    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
071    */
072   public static Warning of(String value) {
073      return new Warning(value);
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Static factory method with a lazy value supplier.
078    *
079    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
080    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public static Warning of(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
083      return new Warning(valueSupplier);
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Creates a new empty {@link Builder}.
088    *
089    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
090    */
091   public static Builder create() {
092      return new Builder();
093   }
094
095   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
096   // Inner types
097   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
098
099   /**
100    * Fluent builder for assembling an HTTP <c>Warning</c> header value.
101    *
102    * <p>
103    * Composes one or more warning-values defined by
104    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7234#section-5.5'>RFC 7234 §5.5</a> into the
105    * <c>warn-code SP warn-agent SP warn-text [SP warn-date]</c> form, with multiple warnings <c>,</c>-separated.  The
106    * 3-digit warn-code is zero-padded, the warn-text and optional warn-date are rendered as quoted-strings, and
107    * {@link #add(String)} is a generic escape hatch for a fully pre-rendered warning-value.
108    *
109    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
110    * <p class='bjava'>
111    *    String <jv>value</jv> = Warning.<jsm>create</jsm>()
112    *       .warning(110, <js>"anderson/1.3.37"</js>, <js>"Response is stale"</js>)
113    *       .build();
114    *    <jc>// =&gt; "110 anderson/1.3.37 \"Response is stale\""</jc>
115    * </p>
116    *
117    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
118    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Warning}
119    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7234'>RFC 7234 - HTTP/1.1 Caching</a>
120    * </ul>
121    *
122    * @since 10.0.0
123    */
124   public static class Builder {
125
126      private final List<String> warnings = new ArrayList<>();
127
128      /**
129       * Adds a warning-value (without a warn-date).
130       *
131       * @param code The 3-digit warn-code. Must be in the range 0-999.
132       * @param agent The warn-agent (host:port or pseudonym, e.g. <js>"-"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
133       * @param text The human-readable warn-text. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
134       * @return This object.
135       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code code} is out of range or {@code agent} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
136       */
137      public Builder warning(int code, String agent, String text) {
138         return add(render(code, agent, text, null));
139      }
140
141      /**
142       * Adds a warning-value with a warn-date.
143       *
144       * @param code The 3-digit warn-code. Must be in the range 0-999.
145       * @param agent The warn-agent (host:port or pseudonym, e.g. <js>"-"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
146       * @param text The human-readable warn-text. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
147       * @param date The warn-date (an HTTP-date). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
148       * @return This object.
149       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code code} is out of range or {@code agent}/{@code date} is invalid.
150       */
151      public Builder warning(int code, String agent, String text, String date) {
152         assertArgNotNull("date", date);
153         return add(render(code, agent, text, date));
154      }
155
156      /**
157       * Generic escape hatch for adding a fully pre-rendered warning-value verbatim.
158       *
159       * @param value The pre-rendered warning-value. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
160       * @return This object.
161       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
162       */
163      public Builder add(String value) {
164         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
165         var v = value.trim();
166         if (v.isEmpty())
167            throw illegalArg("warning value must not be blank");
168         warnings.add(v);
169         return this;
170      }
171
172      private static String render(int code, String agent, String text, String date) {
173         if (code < 0 || code > 999)
174            throw illegalArg("warn-code must be in the range 0-999: {0}", code);
175         assertArgNotNull("agent", agent);
176         assertArgNotNull("text", text);
177         var a = agent.trim();
178         if (a.isEmpty())
179            throw illegalArg("warn-agent must not be blank");
180         var sb = new StringBuilder();
181         sb.append(String.format("%03d", code)).append(' ').append(a).append(' ').append(q(text));
182         if (date != null)
183            sb.append(' ').append(q(date));
184         return sb.toString();
185      }
186
187      private static String q(String value) {
188         var sb = new StringBuilder(value.length() + 2);
189         sb.append('"');
190         for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
191            var c = value.charAt(i);
192            if (c == '\\' || c == '"')
193               sb.append('\\');
194            sb.append(c);
195         }
196         sb.append('"');
197         return sb.toString();
198      }
199
200      /**
201       * Builds the rendered <c>Warning</c> header value.
202       *
203       * <p>
204       * Returns an empty string when no warnings have been registered.
205       *
206       * @return The header value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
207       */
208      public String build() {
209         return String.join(", ", warnings);
210      }
211
212      /**
213       * Builds a {@link Warning} header bean directly from this builder.
214       *
215       * @return A {@link Warning} carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
216       */
217      public Warning toHeader() {
218         return Warning.of(build());
219      }
220
221      @Override
222      public String toString() {
223         return build();
224      }
225   }
226}
